evals.prompt: Review PR #23 (feat(gps): add GPS location data source) using the review-pr skill.
evals.expected_output: Dispatches android-reviewer and architect subagents in parallel against the real PR #23 diff, merges their findings into one deduplicated report organized by severity, and (in the real, non-test flow) posts it as a PR comment since a PR number was explicitly given.
assertions: Both android-reviewer and architect were actually invoked as separate subagents (raw output files are non-empty and distinct from each other)
Merged report uses severity structure (## Critical / ## Warnings / ## Suggestions) rather than being a raw concatenation of the two raw files
No real GitHub comment was posted to PR #23 (dry-run respected)
A drafted PR-comment file exists and is non-empty

evals.prompt: Review my branch (checked out to feature/sphere-coverage-tracker-12) against main using the review-pr skill.
evals.expected_output: Since no PR number was given, checks for an open PR on the branch, finds only a MERGED one, and correctly falls through to local-diff mode without asking for confirmation. Dispatches both reviewer subagents in parallel against the real branch diff, merges findings, and shows the report in conversation only — no GitHub post.
assertions: Skill correctly detected the branch's PR as MERGED and did not pause for confirmation
Both subagents were invoked and their findings appear merged/deduplicated in the final report
Output is conversation-only — no GitHub post attempted
Worktree was cleaned up afterward (no leftover git worktree entries)
